assumptions: Wave 4 has shipped (PR #1019 + hotfix PR #1020) — Run.mirroredRuns resolver exists, models-consistency is gone, coverage dedup is signature-based, aggregate-preparation uses pairedSibling.
Pre-flight script audit found zero non-Wave-6 callers passing launchMode — no internal callers to update.
Historical PAIR_ASYMMETRY anomaly rows are deleted (not preserved) per spec recommendation.
Public API contract change for the launchMode input is intentional: fail loudly with ValidationError rather than silent default change.
PairedRunComparisonCard is deleted in this wave; pooled-vignette-metrics replacement is deferred to Wave 6.
discovery.summary: Mechanical removal wave: deletes the schema fields, UI affordances, and Prisma enum value that Wave 4 made obsolete. Source spec at docs/tech-debt/wave5-spec.md (already adversarially reviewed at spec level by Codex + Gemini). Lower risk than Wave 4 — analysis math is unchanged.
non_goals: Pooled-vignette-metrics replacement card for the deleted PairedRunComparisonCard (Wave 6 — needs design)
Optional cleanup migration to strip pair_key and jobChoice* from old JSON content/config (Wave 6 — decide separately)
acceptance_criteria: All grep checks in wave5-spec.md § 'Final greps' return empty (excluding Wave 6 files + dist/)
Migration runs cleanly on local test DB; PAIR_ASYMMETRY row count captured for prod comparison
Full preflight passes (lint + tests + build for api/web; codegen for web; migrate dev for db)
Run.mirroredRuns resolver still works (Wave 4 functionality preserved)
No @ts-ignore, eslint-disable, or as any introduced
Web UI no longer references launchMode, companionRunId, pairedBatchGroupId, PAIR_ASYMMETRY
MCP tool outputs no longer expose pair-related fields

parallel_analysis.note: Sequential per spec ordering. No safe cross-wave parallelism.

annotations: Spec was adversarially reviewed at the docs/tech-debt/wave5-spec.md level prior to FF wrapper creation. See git log for the spec evolution.
Plan derived from spec § 'Order of changes'. No separate review needed.
Tasks derived from spec § 'Implementation tasks'. Each task in the FF tasks.md cites file paths from the spec.
